what_it_does: This file is a minimal ledger attachment record that registers an image asset against the design-source channel, giving it an id, an attachment type, a target URI, and an added timestamp.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, with no body content
id follows the att- prefix pattern: att-cb7b7191
target uses an image:// URI pointing to design-source/presentation-15p/p-03
added timestamp is ISO 8601 with microsecond precision
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata
target uses a custom image:// URI scheme
timestamp is ISO 8601 with timezone and microseconds
File is otherwise empty after the frontmatter
concerns: The image:// target cannot be resolved or validated from this file alone
No caption, title, or description for the image is provided
No author or provenance field is present
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry that points an image asset (presentation-15p slide p-03) into the design-source channel.
questions: What content is shown in presentation-15p/p-03?
Who added this attachment and why?
How is the image:// URI resolved to an actual file or blob?
Are there related attachments for other slides in presentation-15p?
purpose_vs_actual: No explicit purpose is stated; the fields perform an attachment-registration role, which matches the file's actual content.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to catalog an image asset—specifically slide p-03 from a 15-page presentation—so it can be referenced as an attachment in the design-source channel.
novelty: The target field uses an image:// URI scheme rather than a filesystem path, and the timestamp carries microsecond precision.
contribution: It contributes a precise, timestamped identity and locator for an image attachment in the design-source channel, supporting retrieval and association without embedding the asset itself.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment ledger entry with id att-cb7b7191 linking the design-source channel to image://design-source/presentation-15p/p-03, recorded at 2026-06-22T11:31:01.304518+00:00.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to register slide p-03 of presentation-15p as an image attachment in the design-source channel.
